response.justification: Bogdan brings rare and directly relevant geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and Green Map work, plus demonstrated understanding of distributed systems and real-time data processing from his Dunnhumby microservices background. However, his professional C++ experience is 18 years old (2007–2008 at National Instruments); his recent decade has been primarily TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. For a role where C++ is the primary technology, this recency gap is significant, though his proven ability to learn new stacks and his systems-level thinking could mitigate the risk if he commits to a C++ refresh.
strengths: Direct geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) and Green Map work; understands the specific data structures and challenges of mapping systems
Demonstrated distributed systems and real-time processing knowledge from Dunnhumby role (microservices handling millions of requests/second, async I/O, caching, low-latency requirements)
Clear pattern of expanding expertise across technical stacks and willingness to learn new languages and domains; regularly picks the right tool for the problem
concerns: Professional C++ experience is 18 years old; recent decade has been TypeScript/JavaScript/DLang. No evidence of modern C++ (C++11/14/17/20), which is essential for a primary C++ role
No navigation or routing-specific experience; GISCollective is for ecological data collection, not navigation guidance. Missing domain depth in the specific problem space
No demonstrated experience designing or operating large-scale data pipeline architecture, ETL systems, or tile-generation pipelines at production scale

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and real-time processing experience
